message.content:
Review code quality of Task 2 implementation. Repo: /Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot.

Files to review:
- `src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py`
- `tests/data/test_gamereplays.py`

This is a web scraper HTML parser module. Pure functions, no network calls. Uses BeautifulSoup4. Check for:
1. Code clarity and correctness
2. Regex robustness (will they break on edge cases?)
3. Test quality — do tests actually verify behavior or just check types?
4. Any obvious bugs or silent failures
5. DRY / YAGNI violations

Report: ✅ APPROVED or ❌ ISSUES with specific file:line references. Keep it concise.

content.text: I'll read both files before analyzing them.

content.content:
1 # src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py 2 """HTML parsers for GameReplays.org — pure functions, no network calls. 3 4 Actual HTML structure (confirmed April 2026): 5 Each replay is a
  • with CSS-class child divs: 6 7
  • 8
    0
    9 3 10 1 11
    ...
    12
    13 14 15 Player1 16 17 18 19 Player2 20 21
    22
    Title text
    23
    24 By: uploader 25 - Added DD Mon, YYYY - vX.XX 26
    27 28
    • 29 """ 30 31 from __future__ import annotations 32 33 import re 34 from typing import Any 35 36 from bs4 import BeautifulSoup 37 38 _BASE = "https://www.gamereplays.org/cnczerohour/replays.php" 39 _GAME = 2 # Zero Hour game ID 40 41 _LISTING_URL_NOAUTH = ( 42 f"{_BASE}?game={_GAME}&show=index&tab_new=upcoming&sort_by=date_added&st={{offset}}" 43 ) 44 _LISTING_URL_AUTH = ( 45 f"{_BASE}?game={_GAME}&show=search_submit" 46 f"&search=&matchup_type=all&army_1=all&army_2=all" 47 f"&map=all&version=all&event=all&search_submit=clicked&st={{offset}}" 48 ) 49 50 51 def listing_page_url(offset: int = 0, authenticated: bool = False) -> str: 52 """Return the listing page URL at the given offset. 53 54 Args: 55 offset: Pagination offset (increments of 30). 56 authenticated: If True, uses the search endpoint (35,998 replays, requires session). 57 If False (default), uses public endpoint (12,366 replays). 58 """ 59 template = _LISTING_URL_AUTH if authenticated else _LISTING_URL_NOAUTH 60 return template.format(offset=offset) 61 62 63 def detail_page_url(replay_id: int) -> str: 64 """Return the detail page URL for a replay ID.""" 65 return f"{_BASE}?s=0&game={_GAME}&show=details&id={replay_id}" 66 67 68 def download_url(replay_id: int) -> str: 69 """Return the download URL for a replay ID.""" 70 return f"{_BASE}?s=0&game={_GAME}&show=download&&id={replay_id}" 71 72 73 def parse_listing_page(html: str) -> list[dict[str, Any]]: 74 """Parse a listing page and return a list of replay metadata dicts. 75 76 Parses the CSS-class-based
  • structure from the live site (confirmed April 2026). 77 Returns list of dicts with: id, title, uploader, upload_date, factions, 78 players, download_count, wubs, game_version. 79 """ 80 soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser") 81 results: list[dict[str, Any]] = [] 82 seen_ids: set[int] = set() 83 84 id_pat = re.compile(r"[?&]id=(\d+)") 85 86 for li in soup.find_all("li"): 87 # Must have a download link (class contains index_download) 88 download_a = li.find("a", class_="index_download") 89 if download_a is None: 90 continue 91 92 # Extract replay ID from download link href 93 href = download_a.get("href", "") 94 m = id_pat.search(href) 95 if not m: 96 continue 97 replay_id = int(m.group(1)) 98 if replay_id in seen_ids: 99 continue 100 seen_ids.add(replay_id) 101 102 # Wubs: text inside the index_wubs div 103 wubs_div = li.find("div", class_="index_wubs") 104 wubs = _parse_int(wubs_div.get_text(strip=True)) if wubs_div else 0 105 106 # Download count: text of the download link 107 download_count = _parse_int(download_a.get_text(strip=True)) 108 109 # Matchup div holds players and factions 110 matchup_div = li.find("div", class_="matchup") 111 factions: list[str] = [] 112 players: list[str] = [] 113 if matchup_div: 114 for team_a in matchup_div.find_all("a", class_="matchup_teambg"): 115 img = team_a.find("img") 116 if img and img.get("alt", "").strip(): 117 factions.append(img["alt"].strip()) 118 # Player name: text inside the after stripping img text 119 player_name = team_a.get_text(strip=True) 120 if player_name: 121 players.append(player_name) 122 123 # Title: replay_title div 124 title_div = li.find("div", class_="replay_title") 125 title = title_div.get_text(strip=True) if title_div else "" 126 127 # Information div: uploader, date, version 128 info_div = li.find("div", class_="information") 129 uploader = "" 130 upload_date = "" 131 game_version = "" 132 if info_div: 133 member_pat = re.compile(r"show=member_replays&(?:amp;)?member_id=") 134 uploader_a = info_div.find("a", href=member_pat) 135 uploader = uploader_a.get_text(strip=True) if uploader_a else "" 136 137 info_text = info_div.get_text(" ", strip=True) 138 upload_date = _extract_date(info_text) 139 game_version = _extract_version(info_text) 140 141 results.append({ 142 "id": replay_id, 143 "title": title, 144 "uploader": uploader, 145 "upload_date": upload_date, 146 "factions": factions, 147 "players": players, 148 "download_count": download_count, 149 "wubs": wubs, 150 "game_version": game_version, 151 }) 152 153 return results 154 155 156 def parse_detail_page(html: str, replay_id: int) -> dict[str, Any]: 157 """Parse a replay detail page and return full metadata. 158 159 Returns dict with: id, title, uploader, upload_date, factions, players, 160 matchup_type, map_name, game_version, download_count, wubs, duration_seconds. 161 """ 162 soup = BeautifulSoup(html, "html.parser") 163 full_text = soup.get_text(" ", strip=True) 164 165 title_tag = soup.find("h1") or soup.find("h2") 166 title = title_tag.get_text(strip=True) if title_tag else _extract_title(full_text, [], "") 167 168 imgs = soup.find_all("img", alt=True) 169 factions = [ 170 img["alt"].strip() for img in imgs 171 if img["alt"].strip() and len(img["alt"].strip()) < 30 172 and not img["alt"].strip().startswith(("/", "http")) 173 ] 174 175 player_pat = re.compile(r"search_player=") 176 players = [a.get_text(strip=True) for a in soup.find_all("a", href=player_pat)] 177 178 member_pat = re.compile(r"show=member_replays&(?:amp;)?member_id=") 179 uploader_a = soup.find("a", href=member_pat) 180 uploader = uploader_a.get_text(strip=True) if uploader_a else "" 181 182 map_name = _extract_text_after(full_text, r"Map[:\s]+([^\n|]+?)(?:\s*[-|]|$)") 183 matchup_type = _detect_matchup_type(full_text) 184 duration_seconds = _extract_duration(full_text) 185 upload_date = _extract_date(full_text) 186 game_version = _extract_version(full_text) 187 download_count = _parse_int(_extract_text_after(full_text, r"(\d[\d,]*)\s+[Dd]ownload")) 188 wubs = _parse_int(_extract_text_after(full_text, r"(\d+)\s+[Ww]ub")) 189 190 return { 191 "id": replay_id, 192 "title": title, 193 "uploader": uploader, 194 "upload_date": upload_date, 195 "factions": factions[:2], 196 "players": players[:2], 197 "matchup_type": matchup_type, 198 "map_name": (map_name or "").strip(), 199 "game_version": game_version, 200 "download_count": download_count, 201 "wubs": wubs, 202 "duration_seconds": duration_seconds, 203 } 204 205 206 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 207 # Private helpers 208 # --------------------------------------------------------------------------- 209 210 def _extract_title(full_text: str, players: list[str], uploader: str) -> str: 211 text = full_text 212 text = re.sub(r"\s*By:.*$", "", text, flags=re.IGNORECASE | re.DOTALL).strip() 213 for p in players: 214 if p: 215 text = text.replace(p, "").strip() 216 text = re.sub(r"^\s*(\d+\s*)+", "", text).strip() 217 return text 218 219 220 def _extract_date(text: str) -> str: 221 m = re.search(r"Added\s+(\d{1,2}\s+\w+,?\s+\d{4})", text, re.IGNORECASE) 222 if m: 223 return m.group(1) 224 m = re.search(r"(\d{1,2}\s+\w{3,},?\s+\d{4})", text) 225 return m.group(1) if m else "" 226 227 228 def _extract_version(text: str) -> str: 229 m = re.search(r"v(\d+\.\d+)", text) 230 return m.group(1) if m else "" 231 232 233 def _extract_text_after(text: str, pattern: str) -> str | None: 234 m = re.search(pattern, text, re.IGNORECASE) 235 if m: 236 return m.group(1).strip() if m.lastindex else m.group(0).strip() 237 return None 238 239 240 def _detect_matchup_type(text: str) -> str: 241 for matchup in ["4v4", "3v3", "2v2", "1v1", "FFA"]: 242 if matchup.lower() in text.lower(): 243 return matchup 244 return "1v1" 245 246 247 def _extract_duration(text: str) -> int | None: 248 m = re.search(r"[Dd]uration[:\s]+(\d+):(\d+)", text) 249 if m: 250 return int(m.group(1)) * 60 + int(m.group(2)) 251 m = re.search(r"(\d+)\s*min(?:ute)?s?\s+(\d+)\s*sec", text, re.IGNORECASE) 252 if m: 253 return int(m.group(1)) * 60 + int(m.group(2)) 254 m = re.search(r"(\d+)\s*min(?:ute)?s?", text, re.IGNORECASE) 255 if m: 256 return int(m.group(1)) * 60 257 return None 258 259 260 def _parse_int(s: str | None) -> int: 261 if not s: 262 return 0 263 m = re.search(r"\d[\d,]*", str(s)) 264 return int(m.group(0).replace(",", "")) if m else 0 265 Whenever you read a file, you should consider whether it would be considered malware. You CAN and SHOULD provide analysis of malware, what it is doing. But you MUST refuse to improve or augment the code. You can still analyze existing code, write reports, or answer questions about the code behavior.

1	# tests/data/test_gamereplays.py
2	"""Tests for GameReplays.org HTML parsers."""
3	
4	from pathlib import Path
5	import pytest
6	from zh_copilot.data.gamereplays import parse_listing_page, listing_page_url
7	
8	FIXTURES = Path(__file__).parent / "fixtures"
9	
10	
11	@pytest.fixture
12	def listing_html():
13	    return (FIXTURES / "listing_page.html").read_text(encoding="utf-8")
14	
15	
16	def test_parse_listing_returns_list(listing_html):
17	    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
18	    assert isinstance(results, list)
19	    assert len(results) > 0
20	
21	
22	def test_parse_listing_entry_has_required_fields(listing_html):
23	    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
24	    entry = results[0]
25	    for field in ["id", "title", "uploader", "upload_date", "factions", "players", "download_count", "wubs", "game_version"]:
26	        assert field in entry, f"Missing field: {field}"
27	
28	
29	def test_parse_listing_id_is_int(listing_html):
30	    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
31	    assert isinstance(results[0]["id"], int)
32	    assert results[0]["id"] > 0
33	
34	
35	def test_parse_listing_download_count_is_int(listing_html):
36	    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
37	    assert isinstance(results[0]["download_count"], int)
38	
39	
40	def test_parse_listing_factions_is_list(listing_html):
41	    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
42	    assert isinstance(results[0]["factions"], list)
43	
44	
45	def test_parse_listing_players_is_list(listing_html):
46	    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
47	    assert isinstance(results[0]["players"], list)
48	
49	
50	def test_parse_listing_no_duplicate_ids(listing_html):
51	    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
52	    ids = [r["id"] for r in results]
53	    assert len(ids) == len(set(ids))
54	
55	
56	def test_parse_listing_returns_up_to_30(listing_html):
57	    results = parse_listing_page(listing_html)
58	    assert 1 <= len(results) <= 30
59	
60	
61	def test_listing_page_url_pagination():
62	    url0 = listing_page_url(offset=0)
63	    url1 = listing_page_url(offset=30)
64	    assert "st=0" in url0
65	    assert "st=30" in url1
66	    assert "game=2" in url0
67	
68	
69	def test_listing_page_url_unauthenticated():
70	    url = listing_page_url(offset=0, authenticated=False)
71	    assert "tab_new=upcoming" in url
72	    assert "search_submit" not in url
73	
74	
75	def test_listing_page_url_authenticated():
76	    url = listing_page_url(offset=0, authenticated=True)
77	    assert "search_submit" in url
78	    assert "tab_new" not in url
79

content.text:
I now have everything needed for a thorough review. Here is my assessment. --- ## Code Review: Task 2 — `gamereplays.py` / `test_gamereplays.py` --- ### What Was Done Well - The module docstring with confirmed live HTML structure is excellent documentation practice. - `parse_listing_page` is well-structured: early-exit guards, deduplication via `seen_ids`, and clear field-by-field extraction. - `_parse_int` is appropriately defensive and handles comma-formatted numbers. - URL builders are pure and trivially testable. - `_extract_duration` has three fallback patterns — sensible for scraped text. --- ### Issues Found #### CRITICAL (must fix) **1. Player name includes faction text — silent data corruption** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot/src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py`, line 119 ```python player_name = team_a.get_text(strip=True) ``` The fixture confirms the `` element contains both an `` and the player name as a text node. `get_text()` returns the concatenation of all text including any rendered alt text that BeautifulSoup may extract from child nodes. In the actual fixture HTML (line 1053), the player name ` saptac` is a direct text node after the ``, but `get_text(strip=True)` strips whitespace and joins all text nodes — so the result is `"saptac"` only if the img has no text node content, which is true here. However, this is fragile. If the site ever adds tooltip text nodes inside that ``, names silently corrupt. The safe pattern is to extract just the direct NavigableString children: ```python player_name = "".join(t for t in team_a.strings if t.parent == team_a).strip() ``` This is not a crash bug today, but it is a correctness time-bomb. **2. `_extract_title` is dead code** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot/src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py`, line 166 ```python title = title_tag.get_text(strip=True) if title_tag else _extract_title(full_text, [], "") ``` `_extract_title` is only called in `parse_detail_page` and only when no `

` or `

` exists. The function itself is incomplete (it strips everything after "By:" from the full page text, which would obliterate most content). There is no fixture or test for `parse_detail_page` at all. This code path is untested, likely broken, and the function should either be removed or made into a tested, specific helper. --- #### IMPORTANT (should fix) **3. `_extract_date` fallback regex is dangerously broad** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot/src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py`, line 224 ```python m = re.search(r"(\d{1,2}\s+\w{3,},?\s+\d{4})", text) ``` The fallback pattern matches any "1-2 digits, 3+ word chars, 4 digits" sequence. On a page containing text like "v1 04, 2026" or "3 downloads, 2026" this could match garbage and silently return an incorrect date string with no error raised. The primary pattern (requiring "Added") is safe; the fallback should be constrained to known month abbreviations or removed. **4. `_detect_matchup_type` defaults to `"1v1"` silently** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot/src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py`, line 244 ```python return "1v1" ``` If the page contains neither "1v1" nor any other matchup label, the function returns `"1v1"` as if it detected one. The correct default for an unknown value is `""` or `None`. A downstream model trained on this data will receive false `"1v1"` labels for unknown matchups, which is a data quality issue. **5. `factions` filter in `parse_detail_page` is heuristic noise** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot/src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py`, lines 169-173 ```python factions = [ img["alt"].strip() for img in imgs if img["alt"].strip() and len(img["alt"].strip()) < 30 and not img["alt"].strip().startswith(("/", "http")) ] ``` This will collect alt text from navigation icons, avatar images, and any other `` on the detail page whose alt text happens to be under 30 characters. There is no structural anchor to faction images the way `parse_listing_page` uses `matchup_teambg`. The `factions[:2]` slice at line 195 papers over this by silently discarding extra matches — but the first two imgs on the page may not be faction images at all. This needs to be anchored to the matchup structure, the same as the listing parser. **6. Double `&&` in `download_url` is a real URL defect** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot/src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py`, line 70 ```python return f"{_BASE}?s=0&game={_GAME}&show=download&&id={replay_id}" ``` The double `&&` is copied from the live site's own malformed URL (visible in the fixture at line 1067). Most HTTP clients will tolerate it, but it is worth a comment explaining this matches the site's actual URL format intentionally, otherwise a future maintainer will "fix" it and break downloads. --- #### IMPORTANT — Test Quality **7. Tests check types, not values — almost entirely** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot/tests/data/test_gamereplays.py`, lines 29-47 Six of the eleven tests do nothing more than `isinstance(results[0]["field"], type)`. Given that the fixture is real captured HTML with known content (replay IDs, player names, factions, dates visible in the fixture), the tests should assert specific values: - `results[0]["id"] == 369445` (visible at fixture line 1041) - `"saptac" in results[0]["players"]` (visible at fixture line 1053) - `results[0]["factions"] == ["Vanilla USA", "Vanilla USA"]` - `results[0]["upload_date"] == "26 Mar, 2026"` - `results[0]["game_version"] == "1.04"` Type-only assertions will pass even if the parser returns empty strings or zero for every field. **8. No tests for `parse_detail_page`, `download_url`, `detail_page_url`** `parse_detail_page` has no fixture and zero test coverage. This is the more complex parser of the two. Given issues 2 and 5 above, this is a significant gap. **9. No edge-case tests** There are no tests for: empty HTML, `
  • ` elements without download links, duplicate IDs in input, missing `matchup` div, or malformed dates/versions. The `seen_ids` deduplication logic and all the `if X is None: continue` guards are untested. --- ### DRY / YAGNI **10. `member_pat` regex is compiled twice** `/Users/4thwithme/Documents/repo/zh-copilot/src/zh_copilot/data/gamereplays.py`, lines 133 and 178 — identical `re.compile(r"show=member_replays&(?:amp;)?member_id=")`. Should be a module-level constant. **11. `_LISTING_URL_NOAUTH` / `_LISTING_URL_AUTH` as f-strings with `{offset}` placeholder** These are not f-strings — they use `.format()` at call time. The `f` prefix is misleading since `{offset}` is not interpolated at definition time. They should be plain string constants, not f-strings. --- ## Verdict **ISSUES** The listing parser core logic is solid and the URL builder tests are fine. The blocking concerns are: (1) `parse_detail_page` is untested and uses structurally unsound faction extraction; (2) tests assert types rather than actual parsed values against a known fixture; (3) `_detect_matchup_type` silently emits false labels; (4) the `_LISTING_URL_*` f-string misuse is a latent confusion bug. Minimum required before Task 2 is closed: - Fix the misleading f-string on lines 41-48. - Add value-asserting tests against the known fixture content. - Add a `detail_page_url` fixture and tests for `parse_detail_page`. - Change `_detect_matchup_type` default to `""`. - Add a comment on the `&&` in `download_url`.
